title: Argentinian Beef Empanadas
description: Classic South American pastries filled with seasoned ground beef, served as a savory snack or dinner.
instructions: In a large pan, cook the ground beef over medium heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion, minced garlic, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pan and continue to cook until the onion is translucent.
Let the mixture cool slightly, then stir in the beaten egg.
In a large bowl, combine the flour and water to form a dough. Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
On a lightly floured surface, use a rolling pin to flatten each ball of dough into a circle.
Place a tablespoon or two of the beef mixture onto the center of each dough circle, then brush the edges with a little water.
Fold the dough in half to form a half-circle and press the edges together to seal the empanada. Use a fork to crimp the edges.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush the tops with a little water.
Bake the empanadas in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
